extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won Stage 10 sprint with textbook Quick-Step lead-out after being boxed twice in previous Vuelta sprints.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step corrected previous sprint failures by keeping Viviani center-road through final curve, avoiding barrier boxing that plagued earlier stages.
Sky and Bora attempted long-range disruption before flamme rouge on gentle downhill, forcing lead-out trains to react early.
GC riders deliberately stripped from sprint finale positioning, suggesting teams prioritizing crash avoidance over stage hunting opportunities.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: Demonstrates tactical learning after two failed sprints (one lost, one team error), specifically correcting positioning through curves.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Present in finale but Quick-Step's corrected positioning neutralized his typical barrier-side squeeze tactics.
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: neutral: Positioned himself early without Quick-Step lead-out support, suggesting isolation forced premature move.
